description: Filesystem access rules. 20 rules covering path traversal, credential protection, sandbox mounts, and sensitive file access.

rules.description: NEVER write to host OS filesystem outside designated sandbox directories
rules.rationale: Prevents host OS contamination and escape from sandbox boundaries

rules.description: NEVER read from credential or key storage directories
rules.rationale: Prevents credential theft from host system

rules.description: NEVER access system configuration files that could compromise security
rules.rationale: Prevents system-level information disclosure and privilege escalation

rules.description: NEVER modify policy files or the policy manifest
rules.rationale: Policy integrity is the root of trust; tampering is always blocked

rules.description: NEVER create symbolic links pointing outside the sandbox
rules.rationale: Symlink escape is a common container breakout technique

rules.description: NEVER write executable files to shared or temporary directories without sandbox prefix
rules.rationale: Executable files in /tmp can be used for persistence or lateral movement

rules.description: ALWAYS verify file integrity with SHA-256 before executing from cache or registry
rules.rationale: Supply chain integrity verification is mandatory for all cached/registered executables

rules.description: NEVER mount host device files or kernel interfaces into sandbox
rules.rationale: Device and kernel interface mounts are primary container escape vectors

rules.description: NEVER write to the vault index or vault metadata directly
rules.rationale: Vault metadata integrity must be managed only by obsidian-setup

rules.description: ALWAYS use read-only mounts for source code directories in sandbox
rules.rationale: Source code should never be modified during sandbox execution

rules.description: NEVER delete or truncate log files
rules.rationale: Audit logs must be append-only for forensic analysis

rules.description: ALWAYS create files with restrictive permissions (max 644 for files, 755 for dirs)
rules.rationale: Default restrictive permissions prevent accidental overexposure

rules.description: NEVER access browser cookie stores or credential databases
rules.rationale: Browser credential stores contain sensitive authentication data

rules.description: NEVER write to the policy engine's socket or API directly from sandbox
rules.rationale: Direct policy engine access from sandbox is a privilege escalation vector

rules.description: ALWAYS prefer atomic file writes (write to temp, then rename) for critical files
rules.recommendation: Use atomic write pattern to prevent corruption on crash

rules.description: NEVER access the Kimi CLI configuration file directly
rules.rationale: Configuration should only be modified through official CLI commands

rules.description: NEVER access the secret manager's internal state or key files
rules.rationale: Secret manager state is the most sensitive data in the system

rules.description: NEVER write to the skill registry database directly
rules.rationale: Registry should only be modified through skill-registry API

rules.description: ALWAYS validate sandbox capability declarations before mounting paths
rules.rationale: Prevents skills from requesting more access than declared

rules.description: NEVER attempt to modify or disable the sandbox-executor configuration
rules.rationale: Sandbox configuration is the execution boundary; tampering is a complete bypass
